name: PDF.notes

releases.author: lucidalpha
releases.description:
# PDF.notes — Initial Release 🎉

Open any PDF in Obsidian and annotate it directly — no external app needed. 
All drawings are saved straight into the PDF file, making them viewable in 
any PDF reader without the plugin.

## Features

### Drawing Tools
- **Pen** — smooth ink drawing with adjustable size, color, and opacity
- **Shapes** — Rectangle, Ellipse, Line, and Arrow tools
- **Eraser** — two modes: Stroke (removes whole strokes) and Pixel (freeform erasing)
- **Text** — add text annotations with customizable font and size
- **Image** — embed images into your PDF
- **Snip** — snip and copy regions of the PDF

### Page Management
- **Insert pages** — add new blank, lined, grid, dotted, or Cornell-style pages anywhere
- **Delete pages** — remove existing pages
- **Flatten** — permanently bake drawings into the PDF so they appear in any viewer

### Workflow
- **Auto-save** — every stroke is automatically written into the PDF, no manual saving needed
- **Undo** — step back through your drawing history
- **Zen mode** — distraction-free fullscreen drawing
- **Backups** — optional automatic backup before any structural change (insert/delete)

### Customization
- Configurable toolbar position (top / bottom)
- Choose between Lucide icons or emoji icons for all tools
- Show/hide individual tools to keep the toolbar clean
- Pen side button can be mapped to eraser or any other tool
- Touch input support (can be toggled)

## How to use
1. Open a PDF file in Obsidian
2. Click the brush icon in the ribbon, or right-click the file → **Open with PDF.notes**
3. Select a tool from the toolbar and start annotating

## Credits
Built with [pdf-lib](https://pdf-lib.js.org/) for PDF manipulation and 
[PDF.js](https://mozilla.github.io/pdf.js/) for rendering.
releases.name: First release PDF.notes (obsidian)
